sourceMovie.title: Earthquake
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Earthquake (1974) as a action theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The action and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: San Andreas
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Earthquake clicked for you, San Andreas (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Earthquake?
faq.answer: Try San Andreas, Dante's Peak, Street Kings—each shares DNA with Earthquake while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Earthquake?
faq.answer: Earthquake (1974) is best described as Action, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Earthquake worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
